spells: *The Pus-Whistle ('Genysipp')* - Summons carrion flies from a corpse's wounds.
*Wiege's Cradling of the Stillborn Word ('Halvaszletet sző ringatsa')* - Preserves a dying man's final unspoken sentence.
*The Carrion Flute Orison ('Csontfurulja imma')* - Plays a skeleton's ribs to command lesser dead.
*Csontsuttogás, the Meaty Whisper ('Csonsuttogs hússusogás')* - Interrogates fresh meat for memories of its butcher.
*The Teeth-in-Teeth Incantation ('Foga fogbanj igézet')* - Grafts a victim's teeth inside their own jaw.
*Halottmester's Hanging-Shape ('Halotmestr akaszttalak')* - Raises a corpse suspended, swaying as a patrol sentry.
*The Orphan Syllable ('Árvja szotgg')* - Severs a name from its owner's soul-tether.
*Maggot-Count ('Kukkacszamlás')* - Ages a corpse by tallying its unseen larvae.
*Gristlesinger's First Words ('Porcdalnok elsző szavak')* - Teaches newborn skeletons to chew through restraints.
*The Suppurating Sign ('Gennjező jell')* - Reopens healed wounds on any nearby undead.
*A Widowflesh Sign ('Özvegyhus jeel')* - Causes recent widows' skin to weep black milk.
*Nagaire's spell of the Swallowing Sword ('Nagaire nyellő kardj')* - Draws an enemy's blade into their own throat.
*The Wormlight Figure ('Féreggfénj alakk')* - Lights a chamber by illuminating subcutaneous parasites.
*Scab-Split Chant ('Vartépő éenk')* - Bursts old wounds on a hundred soldiers simultaneously.
*Fifth Tongue ('Ötödk njelv')* - Grants speech to a corpse missing its jaw.
*The Softening of Velvet Organs ('Bársonyszrvek lájjítsa')* - Liquefies liver, kidneys, and spleen within the living.
*The Osculum Pharmacopoeia ('Oszkulum csókk kéeplet')* - Transfers a ghost through a mouth-to-mouth kiss.
*Vérszáj's Kissing-Embrocation ('Vérszáj csőkolló kéeplet')* - Drains blood through pressed lips, leaving skin papery.
*Foulcradle's Lullabywork ('Bölcsbűz altattódal')* - Puts patrol-skeletons into dormant standing sleep.
*Rothadásdal's Eleventh Symphony ('Rothadásdal tzenegygyedk szimfönja')* - Accelerates decay in ten nearby corpses at once.
*Lich's Choking Psalm ('Lich fojjtő zsoltárr')* - Fills a mancer's throat with stolen grave dirt.
*Drowning Diagram ('Fullldás ábrra')* - Floods a skeleton's ribcage with phantom lake water.
*Nigredo Laryngeal ('Fekette gégeffő')* - Silences spoken Deadspeech within a small chamber.
*The Lung-Opening Canticle of the Children of Ratwater ('Patknyvíz tüdönjitó éenk')* - Parts flooded lungs to let a drowned corpse speak.
*The Gummy Syllable ('Raggadós szottag')* - Fuses the lips of a single listener shut.
*A Daughter's Invocation ('Lánj idezees')* - Commands corpses of women who bore children.
*A Kriecher's Gutter-Name ('Kriecher csatorrnnév')* - Compels vermin to abandon a chosen carcass.
*The Degloved-Tongue Word ('Lenjúzott njelvszó')* - Peels the tongue to expose its buried nerve.
*Halotttánc Shape ('Halotttánc alakk')* - Animates corpses into a brief, binding dance-circle.
*The Blueveined Orison ('Kékers imma')* - Surfaces a victim's veins, rendering them translucent.
*Chokestone Surprise ('Fojttkő meglpees')* - Lodges a tohmbstone shard inside a breathing throat.
*Wicking Utterance ('Kanóckokó kijlens')* - Draws embalming fluid from distant pipeline into a vessel.
*Kukacbeszéd's Seventh Cough ('Kukacbeszéd hettedk köhögss')* - Expels a living worm that repeats overheard secrets.
*Féregnyelv's Bile-Litany ('Féregnyelv epllittánja')* - Vomits stored memories as black, legible bile.
*Face-Song of Sextile Angles ('Kilencszög arcsdala')* - Twists a face into nine contradictory expressions at once.
*The Varrat Cataplasm of Stitches ('Varrat öltteesk kéeplet')* - Sutures two corpses along a shared spine.
*Szemfedő's Closing-Song ('Szemfedő zárréenk')* - Seals a dying man's eyes permanently mid-blink.
*The Stellium Keening Shape ('Kilenszeres siroo alakk')* - Raises nine mourners from one grave to wail.
*The Splintered Psalm ('Szilnkos zsoltárr')* - Shatters bone inside a target without breaking skin.
*The Organ-Siphon Venesection ('Szerszíví kéeplet')* - Drains one organ through a straw of hair.
*Elegy of the Equinox ('Napéjegyenlősg elégija')* - Grants mummy-sight in cold beneath shining stars.
*The Censer Capture ('Füstölő fogás')* - Draws a ghost into smoke, bottles it.
*Shriving ('Gyónntatás')* - Strips ghosts of self, compresses them for use.
*Katabasis ('Kalaábazsz')* - Sinks a victim into recursive Ouroboros dream-layers.
*Petitioned-Dead Projection ('Kéert halottk vettítss')* - Projects a mancer's voice through a distant corpse.
ceremonies_rituals_rites: *Matins Below the Ribcage* - Dawn prayer sung through a slit sternum.
*The Quieting of the Orphan Hour* - Hushing hour when ghost-children cry unfed.
*Kiss the Second Tooth* - Child's pact sealed on a molar.
*Csontvár II: Reprise* - Bone-castle reconsecration after structural marrow-shift.
*Compline of the Second Bride* - Widower's vespers for wife who replaces wife.
*Thrashwake* - Violent mourning dance that exhausts the grief.
*The Midwife-Corpse's Seating* - Installing a dead midwife to receive unborn.
*Leave the Window Open for Him* - Household rite inviting the Old Man Below.
*Do Not Wake the Ash-Mother* - Silent vigil by a crone's cold hearth.
*The Little Mass of Salt, Hair, and Failing Breath* - Hedge-witch blessing spoken over a dying infant.
*Embalmwake* - Drunken vigil held inside fluid-pipeline cisterns.
*Small Sunday* - Weekly child-sized sabbath observed between the true Sundays.
*A Fourth Flensing* - Skinning performed once prior three have failed.
*Kenotaph III: Lamentation* - Third visit to an empty grave, weeping required.
*The Widowing of the Eldest Son* - Son ritually mourned as his mother's new husband.
*Húskapu IV: Threshold* - Fourth passage through a door of bound meat.
*Undergrafting* - Sewing living tissue onto buried corpse-kin.
*Mournfeast* - Gorging performed while naming each dead aloud.
*Mother of Worms' Wedding-Call* - Summons issued to would-be consorts at dusk.
*Szürkevér's Hood-Drawing* - Greyblood initiate's cowl lowered over new eyes.
*The Csontvár Ritual* - Bone-tower consecration using seven mourners' teeth.
*Chryptcrawling* - Pilgrimage made knee-walking through sacked vaults.
*The Second Splinter* - Removing the splinter a dying man left behind.
*Red Wednesday* - Midweek bloodletting honoring the Red King.
*Sepulchration* - Rite sealing a grave against resurrecters' shovels.
*Ordinance of the Fourth Gut* - Legal butchery reserved for the colon.
*Rite Seventeen-B* - Bureaucratic backup ritual when Rite Seventeen fails.
*Wet Feast* - Uncooked meal eaten weeping over the platter.
*Vespers of Ash and Navel* - Evensong whispered into a mother's belly-button.
*Gallowsvigil* - Night spent guarding a hanged man's sway.
*Todtwäscher's Trine Scouring* - Corpse-washer scrubs the dead nine consecutive times.
*The Vitriol Supper of the Coronach Mother of the Ditch* - Roadside meal laid out for a drowning ghost.
*The Opening of the Sickened Cradle* - Heretical attempt to unseal the Thin Man's tohmb.
*Bring Down the Heifer's Daughter* - Slaughter of a calf born to slaughtered kine.
*Statute Decan, Subclause of Ash* - Legal recitation required before cremating a stranger.
*The Flensing of the Borrowed Skin* - Returning stolen hide to its original owner.
*The Eleventh Mourning* - Grief cycle observed eleven full years after death.
*A Harridan's Third Anointing* - Old woman oiled thrice to defer her passing.
*Law Three and One Half* - Half-statute invoked between two contradictory rites.
*The Children's Unburial* - Parents digging up small graves by moonlight.
curses_affliction: *Coppercough* - Sputum tastes of pennies; lungs slowly verdigris inside.
*Cerement-Itch* - Skin crawls as if bound in unseen graveclothes.
*Laughing Sores* - Open wounds giggle softly at passing strangers.
*The Sweetmilk of the Well* - Drawn water curdles sugary; drinkers birth pale worms.
*Lockjaw Gift* - Teeth seal shut, yet prophecies leak through gums.
*The Gift That Feeds* - An extra mouth opens somewhere private, demanding meals.
*Bane From the Cistern* - Bathwater remembers the victim, drowning them nightly in dreams.
*Autolytic Mercy* - Healing wounds turn vinegar; every kindness festers afterward.
*Tallowed-Skin* - Flesh softens to candle-fat; body weeps warm drippings.
*Breath-of-Sevens* - Each exhalation counts down; seventh breath kills.
*The Ague of Twain Dawns* - Fever returns every second sunrise, never truly lifting.
*Sallow Shrift* - Confession yellows the tongue, leaving truth permanently jaundiced.
*Knuckle-Mildew* - Grey mold blooms between fingers; grips rot loose.
*Blue Plague* - Capillaries map cobalt across the living face.
*Obsequy Rot* - Corpse decomposes during its own funeral, finishing before burial.
*Frore Pate* - Skull remains glacier-cold; thoughts freeze mid-sentence.
*Third Yellowing* - After two jaundices, a third gilds the eyes shut.
*The Dumb Ache of the Hanged Wife* - Throat bruise appears with a silent, grieving pressure.
*Eleventh Bedsore* - Tenth heals clean; the eleventh never does.
*Softspine* - Vertebrae turn to wet cartilage; victim droops like wilted stalk.
*Malison of the Weasand* - Gullet narrows until only graveyard soil passes down.
*Ichor-Thirst* - Craving for black blood overrides all other hungers.
*The First Wetness* - An unexplained dampness blooms, preceding every subsequent illness.
*Wizened Dugs* - Breasts shrivel to fig-leather; milk runs grey.
*Brand of the Hungry Saint* - Starvation mark burns on ribs; food passes through untouched.
*Slow Threnody* - One tear yearly, carrying a year's griefs.
*Hex of Syzygy Nails* - Fingernails fall serially; the tenth never grows.
*Greyblood Working* - Blood thickens ashen, clots reluctantly, and chills the limbs.
*A Transit Suppuration* - Eight boils heal; the ninth bursts worms.
*Murrain of the Fifth Ditchling* - Fifthborn child spreads a rot through livestock and kin.
*Macerated Leman* - Lovers taste rancid; affection curdles upon contact.
*Mark of the Spilled Womb* - Belly scar weeps monthly, though no womb remains.
*Fourteenth Sequela* - Thirteen cuts survive; the fourteenth always kills.
*Clavicle Hiss* - A whispered curse escapes the collarbone at night.
*The Grievous Stoop* - Spine folds under the weight of inherited guilt.
*Toad-Lungs* - Breath bubbles wetly; victim croaks instead of speaks.
*Womb of yellow* - Unborn children gestate jaundiced, stillborn, and sulfur-smelling.
*The Kindness From Below* - Buried dead offer gentle dreams, then claim the dreamer.
*The Mute Belly Aching* - Silent gut cramps that never surface as sound.
*The Adipocere Pus of the Fifth Son of the Fifth Son* - Tar weeps from every pore of that doomed lineage.
mundane_necromantic_duties: *Daywatch* - Sunlit vigil keeping fresh corpses from waking early.
*Tally the Fingers* - Counting severed digits in the reagent barrels.
*Cleaning Cerements* - Scraping cartilage and gristle from harvested skeletons.
*Charnel-Hair-Weaving* - Braiding scalp hair into wards against uninterred souls.
*Marrowlaving* - Rinsing marrow cavities to flush necromantic residue.
*Ordinance Seventeen, Minor* - Resealing any chrypt lid opened before dusk.
*Wormwatch* - Guarding Coldsoil pots against escaping corpse-worms.
*Salt the Mouths* - Packing coarse salt behind the teeth of the freshly dead.
*Casting the Runes* - Scratching warding Deadspeech onto threshold stones nightly.
*Chrypcartage* - Hauling sealed chrypt-caskets between vault and road.
*Gristlework* - Trimming tendon-scraps into cordage for lesser bindings.
*A Nail Census* - Cataloguing every fingernail dropped during Shriving.
*Writ Eleven* - Inking a bone-slip for each soul shriven that shift.
*Coldwatch* - Tending the ice that keeps mancer tohmbs dormant.
*Trimming Draughts* - Skimming rind from vats of embalming fluid.
*Bind the Eyelids Shut* - Suturing the lids of cadavers before viewing hours.
*The Seven-Count of Hair* - Counting scalp strands in sevens, discarding stray counts.
*Threshing the Old* - Breaking dry ancestor-bones into dust-sack reagent.
*Diagenetic Compaction* - Pressing fermented ghost-foam into brick for transport.
*Thirdwatch* - The bleak hour-shift between matins and dawnlight.
*A Fourth Hour Counterprayer* - Whispered rebuke against prayers the dead might answer.
*Keeping of the Hairshirts* - Laundering the bristled vestments of junior resurrecters.
*Body-Lading* - Loading carted corpses in proper head-to-foot order.
*Tally of Sixteen Mouths* - Feeding sixteen skull-jaws their nightly spoonful of jelly.
*Bonecartage* - Wheeling barrows of picked skeletons to the shriving house.
*Undressing the Unclaimed* - Stripping the nameless dead for the Tailors.
*The Six Reluctancies* - Reading aloud the six spells no apprentice wants recited.
*Stitching of the Burlap Cautery* - Sewing sackcloth shrouds over torn chest cavities.
*The Price* - Surrendering a personal memory to quiet a restless patrol-skeleton.
*Rendering of Cold Fat* - Boiling down corpse-suet into tallow for banshee-wax.
*Empty the Third Vessel Before Matins* - Pouring the third censer-jar out before first bell.
*Clause Two of the Midnight Obligations* - Reading the dead the hour and their own epitaphs.
*The Forty-Second Toll: in K-major* - Striking the bone-bell in its proper cracked key.
*Combing the Chrypt-Lanes* - Walking the vault corridors, gathering what wandered loose.
*Thirty-First Culling* - Putting down the month's final batch of failed raisings.
*Count the Loose Teeth* - Sorting shed molars from the charnel-floor sweepings.
*Cortina-Oiling* - Greasing joint-sockets of working skeletons against winter seize.
*Cusp Scouring* - The ninth and final scrub of the altar slab before dawn.
*Weasand-Clearing* - Pulling stoppage from corpse-throats so Deadspeech carries clean.
*The Seventeenth Silence* - Holding the seventeenth breath-pause in the night's summoning liturgy.
astrological_religious_events: *Hálókő Eclipse* - Slate sun eaten; golems wake beneath earth.
*Bleedmoon* - Harridan's moon runs red; wounds refuse clotting.
*Chimaera V: Conduit* - Fifth star opens a throat between planes.
*Rozsda Vigil* - Night spent scouring rust from family bones.
*The Gibbet Alignment* - Seven stars hang like nooses in procession.
*Feast of the Breathing Prodrome* - Cultists eat through holes cut in neighbors.
*Vespers of the Cracked Gut* - Evening prayers sung while fasting unto rupture.
*The Shrike Market Hour* - Dead birds barter names for unborn grudges.
*Gristmill Hour* - Dream-hour when ancestors grind teeth into flour.
*Albedo Milk Tide* - Wet-nurses weep ink; infants born already literate.
*Hour of the Kneeling Pig* - Livestock bow toward the Lake of Tomatoes.
*The Wroth Procession* - Flayer's parade; speakers flayed for each syllable.
*Szemfény's Harvest* - Eye-shine reaps truth from the honest sleeper.
*Lampreygate* - River-mouths open; the drowned swim upstream home.
*Sallowsolstice* - Longest jaundiced day; livers quail inside pilgrims.
*Mass of the Fetid Bell* - Cathedral bell tolls pus across three parishes.
*Chimaera IX: Vestibule* - Ninth star's antechamber admits only barefoot corpses.
*Night of the Inverted Choir* - Throats sing downward; melody exits through anus.
*Tűzbéka's Mating* - Firetoads couple; swamps ignite with spawning heat.
*The Grinning Aperture* - A sky-hole opens, teeth ringed around it.
*The Rust Nativity* - Iron statues bleed oil; wrought-iron saints whelp nails.
*The Unspooling of Grandmother Rib* - Ancestral cage unwinds, thread by thread, through descendants.
*Vigil of the Spagyric Marrow* - Long bones seep; vigilkeepers catch the discharge.
*The Electric Oval* - Aurora narrows; hair stands on every grave.
*The Kidneystone Vigil* - Pilgrims pass stones aloud before the altar.
*Eve of Split* - Pregnant animals birth twins already quarreling.
*The Offal Vespers* - Priests recite while sorting yesterday's entrails.
*Groin-Lantern Night* - Hung guts glow; brothels douse their honest candles.
*Sputum: The Conjunction* - Two stars kiss; consumptives cough matched phlegm.
*Chimaera VII: Larynx* - Seventh star hums; every sung oath becomes binding.
*Suture's Equinox* - Day and night stitched; old scars twitch open.
*Feast of Retrograde Goiters* - Nine afflicted pilgrims fed; their swellings speak counsel.
*Viscera Matins* - Dawn prayers mumbled through a freshly opened belly.
*The Cataract Ascendant* - Blind stars rise; seers go milky for hours.
*Seventh Phlebotomy of the Sky* - A fixed gash weeps slate-colored rain all day.
*Procession of Seven Mouths* - Seven heralds shout one word, one apiece, westward.
*Lurid Day* - Sun flares yellow; skin blisters into rude colors.
*Káoszkő's Resonance* - A buried stone hums; compasses forget which way.
*Őrlőkő's Descent* - Millstone star sinks; bread refuses to rise.
*The Umbilic Transit* - A cord of light crosses noon; mothers remember severed kin.
titles_roles: *Gland-Taster* - Samples endocrine juices to diagnose a corpse's sins.
*Cantor Vérskéz* - Chants blood-hymns that thicken fresh wounds into scripture.
*Deacon Rags* - Launders vestments stained by sacramental flaying ceremonies.
*Weenlighter* - Lights candles made from rendered stillborn fat.
*The Tongue* - Speaks for a mancer who forbade his own mouth.
*Cutter of Lesser Ligaments* - Prepares corpses by severing the unneeded sinews.
*Mage of Flies* - Commands maggot-swarms that shrive souls into useful pulp.
*Singer of the Long Teeth* - Lulls harridans' hounds with molar-note lullabies.
*Curate of the Box* - Tends the reliquary coffin housing a Thin-Man splinter.
*Tooth-Warden* - Inventories the molars harvested from executed debtors.
*Sub-prior Würgbein* - Junior abbot who interrogates prisoners via throat-bones.
*Rib-Usher* - Parts cadaver ribcages to seat arriving congregants.
*Polite Flayer of Honored Guests* - Skins visiting dignitaries with practiced, courteous precision.
*Almoner of the Slow Pus* - Doles festering salves to lepers outside the Leper Stone.
*The Barren Obstetrix* - Midwife who delivers nothing but stillborn homunculi.
*Jackal* - Outrunner who scavenges battlefields for still-warm mancer material.
*The Chancellor of Opened Graves* - Notarizes every exhumation warrant issued along the Road.
*Groom of the Sclerotium Stall* - Feeds and grooms the chapel's undead stallions.
*Meatnurse* - Suckles torture victims back to sufferable consciousness.
*Lord Chamberlain of Kindnesses* - Oversees mercy killings within noble households.
*Nail-Confessor* - Hears final confessions while driving iron through palms.
*Spleenreeve* - Assesses spleen-tithes owed by each parish household.
*Friar of the Crooked Leg* - Mendicant monk whose shattered knee grants prophecy.
*Liver-Herald* - Proclaims daily omens read from fresh-split livers.
*Gutkeep* - Warden tasked with guarding chrypt-buried entrails overnight.
*Midwife of Sore* - Coaxes boils and abscesses into ripe weeping.
*Reeve Knochenfleisch* - Magistrate who settles disputes with bonemeal weregild.
*Squire of Leaking Rooms* - Attends a knight whose wounds never stop seeping.
*Baron of the Sixth Mouth* - Minor noble who feeds a surplus parasitic maw.
*Vulture Captain* - Leads a company of corpse-scouring conscripts.
*Archdeacon Bleedhand* - Senior cleric whose palms weep consecrated blood nightly.
*The Tender of Forgotten Wounds* - Dresses injuries the wounded themselves cannot remember.
*Skullgrubber* - Digs battlefield skulls free for the lichen-farmers' hymns.
*The Ascendant Widow* - Ritual mourner married successively to eight dead mancers.
*Minister of the Furnishing* - Arranges tohmb interiors with the wealthy dead's belongings.
*Wombwright* - Shapes artificial wombs from leather, wax, and prayer.
*Warden of the Open Chest* - Guards a saint's eternally-splayed ribcage relic.
necromancy_supplies: *Violet Jelly* - Waxen unguent that melts tissue but spares bone.
*Embalming Fluid* - Refinery-piped preservative feeding outlying mancer facilities.
*Banshee-wax* - Staple field-kit wax of uncertain purpose.
*Calcination Dust* - Sack-carried reagent, flour-fine and common.
*Sewer Worm Silk* - Woven rope stronger than iron shackles.
*Ill Omen's Heart* - Vialed substance whispered to foretell doom.
*Tohmbdust* - Harvested tohmb-dust, reagent for sleep toxins.
*Soul Scarabs* - Wooden phylacteries cradling souls in leaded earth.
*Firebug Lantern: Stingerglass* - Caged killing bugs, single-release, repels slugs.
*Song-Singing Fungus* - Red mushroom completing songs, rooting through sleepers.
*Cap-Mouthed Singer* - Smaller fungus shrieking near uncontrolled dead.
*Alabaster Masks* - Twenty-three faces lending borrowed flesh and fate.
*Sorrowful Father Mask* - Leathers the skin; kindles easily to flame.
*Necromancer's Shovel* - Spade that splits marble coffins like loam.
*Reflective Ointment* - Apple-bottled metal draught, cursing skin mirror-bright.
*Hasodeki's Crucible* - Clay kiln-portal, keyed by the word Bindweed.
*Poison Landmine* - Buried device silencing necromancy, lulling the dead.
*Speech Cheese* - Rind granting brief fluency in wisp-tongues.
*Bloodstone* - Pales and shudders near doppelganger-ridden flesh.
*Lemon Cider* - Secret-drunk draught immunizing against ghoul claws.
*Coldsoil* - Pot-soil holding undead worms in preserved half-death.
*Scroll-Armor* - Vellum-lined plate warding most ghosts away.
*Columbine Water* - Driftwood-bucket medium containing ghosts and their Voices.
*Floor Dust* - Dripped into columbine, grants oblivion on request.
*Monkshood Potion* - Silences armor and footfall for sixty-three minutes.
*Quieting Mirror* - Lidless relic stilling thought, curing night terrors.
*Glowbug in Amber* - Un-lifed insect frozen into perpetual lantern.
*Shelley Stone* - Olive-pit relic reviving one throat, only once.
*Lead Curtain* - Threaded sheet no ghost may pass through.
*Engraved Nautilus* - Ivory shell mapping the Stillbreather's tunnel.
*Feral Pickled Fairie* - Limewater corpse retaining residual magic.
*Jugflower* - Pressed blossom shattering into deafening piercing ring.
*Pure Wheat* - Cracker-bread erasing fatigue in a single bite.
*Far Speaking Necklace* - Silver chain touching distant mancers to the ear.
*Emerald with Trapped Soul* - Crystalline gravel-vessel housing a bound soul.
*Whispering Cloth* - Demon-era lining torn into deafening-proof earplugs.
*Mancer's Censer* - Smoking vessel luring ghosts for bottled capture.
*Shriving Press* - Industrial rite compressing ghosts into digestible matter.
*Bindweed* - Verbal key unlocking Hasodeki's kiln-portal.
*Lippergard* - Wood-and-copper mouthguard shielding a mancer's casting tongue.
*Gullet Honey* - Waxy throat-salve soothing raw lips and throat.
*Leather Scrolls* - Cured hide inscribed with written Deadspeech wards.
*Remembered Roots* - Gnarled aged ginger-pieces used to lure wandering souls.
*Hand Kiln* - Belt-slung crucible for fieldside ghost compression.
*Marrow Auger* - Hand-cranked bone-drill tapping resurrection residue for testing.
*Rising Chrism* - Anointing oil priming fresh corpses for raising.
*Hungry Reliquary* - Canopic box, used for holding and preserving organs.
locations: *The Road of Graves* - Endless grave-paved road threading the ruined world.
*The End of the Road* - Unknown terminus whence the Old Man Below issues.
*The Oldvaults* - Ancient necromancer dungeons buried beneath the Road.
*Mool's Chrypt* - Heather-vault maintained by gearslave ape automatons.
*The Sleep Tombs* - Mancers' slumber-vaults guarded by an elder doom.
*Chrypt of Unconsecrated Ashes* - Oldvault where sinners burned and were strewn.
*Abbottoir of the Carnivores* - Oldvault charnel named for its carnivores.
*The Buried Forest* - Oak roots prying apart Road epitaphs.
*Landmine Forest* - Poison-mined woods suppressing necromancy and dormanting undead.
*Wraithcotte Estate* - Ruined manor lying within Landmine Forest.
*Whiteeye Waste* - Black-grey dust-curtain woven from winter pelts.
*Waste Bridge* - Unsupported arc over the Whiteeye canyon.
*The Old Soulwood* - Pre-cataclysm forest grown from concubine's juniper berries.
*Blind Eyes* - Eggshell ponds holed through to an elsewhere ocean.
*Middlemoss* - Oldest town, tohmb-built down a heather-gorge's walls.
*The Athidal* - Sulfurous gorge halving Middlemoss in two.
*The Chine Pit* - Unsanctioned folk-shrine to a local butcher.
*The Lake of Tomatoes* - Red-sheened lake where Gallbladder's mutants worship.
*Deadman's Pile* - Mountain the Road passes through, around, and over.
*The Meathook Mountains* - Glacier range of frozen undead exhaling Winter.
*Lost Glaciertop* - Named peak somewhere in the Meathook range.
*Glacier Canyon* - Ice-cut canyon hosting Crackling Spiders.
*The Massy Fane* - Old Man Below's lost temple within the Meathooks.
*The Tunnel of the Stillbreather* - Underground passage cradling a resting Lich.
*Hornwater* - Canal city south of the Meathooks, guild-ruled.
*Hornwater Lake* - Island-fortressed lake anchoring the city of Hornwater.
*Limbichorous Dunes* - Frigid thousand-mile dunes between Meathooks and Hornwater.
*The Waxy Maize Fields* - Impassable surface obstacle south of the Meathooks.
*The Scarab Mines* - Underground route beneath the Waxy Maize Fields.
*Alsoburg* - Cave-town waypoint on the Scarab Mines route.
*Mellowdepth* - Polyp-lined marble pipe carrying the Road undersea.
*The Leviathan Fields* - Alternate Road route travelers refuse to take.
*The Holt of Old Souls* - Frontier waypoint a hundred leagues past Middlemoss.
*Forest of Candles and Skulls and Roaches and Fungi and Ruptured Capillaries* - Third overland route to Hornwater.
*The Sickened Cradle* - Thin Man's sealed tohmb, sought by prophecy.
*Headstone Mountain* - Landmark rising above the Sickened Cradle.
*The Emerald House* - Lady Horsehair's temple; priestess hierarchy with violence-talents.
*Pilgrim Tohmb* - Roadside shrine dedicated to one necromancer-god.
*Tower of Silence* - Monks of the Lidless' seat in Deep Compost.
*Deep Compost* - Territory housing the Tower of Silence.
*Thistlecrown* - Mountaintop bailey beneath Old Man Below's frozen cathedral.
*Tohmbtown* - Dweller settlement built atop repurposed tohmbs.
*The Rat Farmer's Barn* - Named rural landmark along the Road.
undead: *Birchmen* - Dead birch trees possessed by blood-drinking spirits.
*Ghosts* - Handless incorporeal dead, betrayed by clapping.
*Veyers* - Skull-cavity skeletons inflicting exotic maladies.
*Pumice Veyer* - Veyer hurling pumice like sling bullets.
*Minnow Veyer* - Veyer with a skull cavity teeming with minnows.
*Sun Veyer* - Veyer cradling a small yellow sun.
*Liches* - Necromancers storing their death in remote vessels.
*Starshadow* - Site-bound wraith warden enforcing local curses.
*Gasps* - Water-rising skeletons exhaling frost and stones.
*Gonging Hearse* - Ten-legged bone carriage tolling sensory collapse.
*Gallowspook* - Sackcloth wight stuffed with hazelwood chips.
*Undead Centipede* - Crawler undone only by newborn saliva.
*Bell Ringer* - Cone-mouthed skeleton whose eleven tolls induce sleep.
*Stitched Soul Men* - Variegated-flesh colonists serving the Red King.
*Shambler* - Collapsed resurrection fused into burning tissue.
*Mailed Flayer* - Deathless armored knight guarding Scratcher tohmbs.
*Halo of Worms* - Flatworm colony ringing statue plinths.
*Lectergast* - Sideways-dancing wraith haunting ossuary undersides.
*Living Bones* - Speaking, rhyming skeletal undead.
*Doppelganger Skeletons* - Immortal mancers stealing fresh bodies by spine.
*Mummies* - Beautiful hypnotic undead retaining necromantic skill.
*Ghouls* - Grey-skinned pack dead repelled by lemon cider.
*Wooly Vampire* - Dream-feeder conjuring the victim's thorned shadow.
*Cankarnis* - Rotting head atop regenerating stone statue-body.
*Dust Slimes* - Debris animated by soul-scrap ectoplasm.
*Hunchhobble* - Lurching dust slime variant.
*Wriggling Mass* - Writhing dust slime variant.
*Dropper* - Ceiling-hung dust slime variant.
*False Puddle* - Dust slime mimicking still water.
*Hides* - Flay-clad human slaves of the Crawling Silk.
*Restless Upright Bones* - Generic ambulatory skeletons of the Road.
*Rolling Walrus Bones* - Generic tumbling pinniped undead.
*Vigorous Blood* - Generic animate blood undead.
*Skinrights* - Generic skin-worn undead type.
*Seventh-House-Bells* - Generic undead strongest within ice.
*Zugg* - Evil wraith-ogre lurking the Road.
*Grot* - Innately undead toad-centipede-necromancers in jellyfish leather.
cosmologisms: *The Road of Graves* - Endless road paved with gravestones of every dead person.
*The Slate Sun* - Cold, slate-colored sun providing light but no warmth.
*The Harridan's Moon* - Poxy yellow moon marking the night of witches.
*The Time of Dying* - Present age after necromancers exterminated the demon kings.
*The Plane of Forever-Autumn* - Faraway plane whence fallen, decay-colored leaves arrive on wind.
*The Fifth Star* - Dusk star whose appearance renders Ents unsafe to stand on.
*The Amber Ox* - Named constellation visible from the Lake of Tomatoes region.
*The Green Firebeetle* - Mythological insect whose sky-flight paints the aurora.
*The End of the Road* - Metaphysical terminus whence the Old Man Below issues forth.
*The Sickened Cradle* - The Thin Man's tohmb, whose opening unleashes final entropy.
*The Thin Man* - Sloth-god of sickness and entropy, locked in eternal sleep.
*The Old Man Below* - Pride-god of history and age, dapper roving death-spirit.
*The Scratcher* - Wrath-god of blood and curses, wielder of the Lash.
*The Mother of Worms* - Lust-god of decay, nude vampire beautiful and rotten.
*Gallbladder* - Gluttony-god of consumption, Lord of Lake and the Grootslang.
*Lady Horsehair* - Greed-god of possession, the Porcelain Girl Doll.
*The Red King* - Envy-god of persecution, master of confession racks.
*Emerald Houses* - Lady Horsehair's temples.
*The Chrypt of the Fermented Princess* - Mother of Worms' sacred site of divine punishment.
*The Dwellers* - Descendants of the necromancers' acolytes; modern humanity.
*The Epitaphs* - Gravestone-cobbles binding souls against necromantic harvest.
*Ghost Factories* - Industrial operations processing uninterred souls into necromantic material.
*Deadspeech* - Magical Hungarian-rooted language of necromancy.
*Words of Rotten Syllables* - Profane antique witch-magic surviving outside necromancy.
*Lendendrung* - Copper golem who assassinated Surveillance, first Demon King to fall.
*Surveillance* - First Demon King, felled at the founding war's opening.
epithets: *The Road of Graves* - Epithets: The Long Road, That Road Familiar and Strange, The Procession
*The Slate Sun* - Epithets: The Cold Coin, Widow-Star, Grey Eye of Heaven
*The Harridan's Moon* - Epithets: Yellowed Tooth of Witches, Jaundice-Wheel, Pale-Grinning Harlot
*The Time of Dying* - Epithets: None
*The Plane of Forever-Autumn* - Epithets: The Leaf-Beyond, That Land Where No Green Returns, Hinterfold
*The Fifth Star* - Epithets: The Latecomer, Needle in the Black
*The Amber Ox* - Epithets: The Slow-Burning Bullock, Honey-Horned Warden, The Sweet-Blooded Beast
*The Green Firebeetle* - Epithets: Flight of Phosphor Wings, Meathook's Will-o-Wisp
*The End of the Road* - Epithets: The Last Tohmb, Terminus-Under-Frost, Hem of the World
*The Sickened Cradle* - Epithets: Cot of the Wakeless, Bed of the Silver Mask
*The Thin Man* - Epithets: He Who Sleeps in Fever, The Silver-Masked Sleeper, Dreamer of the Final Entropy
*The Old Man Below* - Epithets: The Roving Cold, Strange Grandfather, Pride of Ages Old
*The Scratcher* - Epithets: The Lash-Bearer, Nail-in-the-Throat
*The Mother of Worms* - Epithets: Beautiful Skinface, Comely Sepulcher
*Gallbladder* - Epithets: The Fat Man, Lord of Lake, His Grand Corpulency
*Lady Horsehair* - Epithets: The Porcelain Girl Doll, The Girl Doll, The Mane-Clad Miser
*The Red King* - Epithets: The Scarlet Warden, The Cruel Crown
*Emerald Houses* - Epithets: Green-Glassed Temples, Dollmaker Cloisters
*The Chrypt of the Fermented Princess* - Epithets: Her Sugared Sepulcher
*The Dwellers* - Epithets: Road Men, Dying Tenants
*The Epitaphs* - Epithets: The Tongueless Ledger, Litany-Under-Boot
*Ghost Factories* - Epithets: Soulmills
*Deadspeech* - Epithets: The Tongue Disinterred
*Words of Rotten Syllables* - Epithets: The Witches' Verses
*Lendendrung* - Epithets: The Copper-Wrought Giant, Soulforged Manslaughter
*Surveillance* - Epithets: Dead King-of-Many-Eyes
